title: Oden’s Past Begins to Define Wano’s Tragedy
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Kawamatsu, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Oden, Unknown person #8 as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: But this is way too hot!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wahahaha!! Hey, cat!! Oden would not be open if it is not boiled and eaten hot!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And what are you meant to be!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: drifted to this country after our ship sank!! When we asked for help...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: People threw stones at us because we were FISHMEN.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My mother was injured, so we were unable to return home.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: With her dying breath my mother told me, "Fishmen are always discriminated against in this world..."

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "You would be more likely to survive if you claimed to be a kappa."

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So now, I am KAWAMATSU THE KAPPA. I live off of the left- overs others throw away.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I am so grateful. Not only did you save me,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: But you fed me delicious food, too.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I will never forget what you did for me today.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Oh no, I forgot my manners.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Now you're acting like an adult!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Hyahaha Ha!! What an interesting cat!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Meeting you guys has made me realise...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That there are many creatures in the world I have yet to see. Now I am really excited for my next voyage!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It should be fine, but we ARE running low on funds.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You lent more money to that Orochi fellow, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Why do you keep doing that!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oh, well... even though he is on his own now, he used to work for Yasuie... so I cannot just leave him to die...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This is the essence of walking like a Ninja.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Why were you trying to steal so much money?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you are going to execute us, then get it over with already!! You hedgehog!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: He sure recruited some odd characters...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I will give you the money you tried to steal....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wait, Lord Yasuie! If you do that, Hakumai will run out of...!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Are you being serious!? Is something wrong with you? Is this some sort of trap!?

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 60 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
